title: [LLVM][SimplifyCFG] Make validLookupTableConstant explicitly reject vector types.
author: paulwalker-arm
description: validLookupTableConstant indirectly rejects vectors through its selection of supported Constant classes. However, this is not sufficient when ContantInt/FP are used to represent constant vector splats and so I've added an explicit bailout.

tests.test_body:
target triple = "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define <4 x float> @no_switch_to_lookup_table_vector_splat(i32 %c) {
entry:
  switch i32 %c, label %default [
    i32 0, label %bb0
    i32 1, label %bb1
    i32 2, label %bb2
  ]

bb0:                                              ; preds = %entry
  br label %ret

bb1:                                              ; preds = %entry
  br label %ret

bb2:                                              ; preds = %entry
  br label %ret

default:                                          ; preds = %entry
  unreachable

ret:                                              ; preds = %bb2, %bb1, %bb0
  %phi = phi <4 x float> [ splat (float 1.000000e+00), %bb0 ], [ splat (float 2.000000e+00), %bb1 ], [ splat (float 3.000000e+00), %bb2 ]
  ret <4 x float> %phi
}

comments.author: paulwalker-arm
comments.body:
> This preserves the existing behavior, but I don't think there is any actual _problem_ with allowing vectors here, right? It should be both valid and profitable.

Agreed, I was just taking my usual paranoid approach :)

> It might make sense to instead add ConstantVectorData to the whitelist (ConstantVector in general would require handling constant expressions inside vectors). Or if we don't do that, should at least leave a TODO to make it clear that this is just preserving historical behavior.

I'd rather keep the two separate so the latter can be reverted without affecting the vector ConstantInt/FP transition.  I've created https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/183057 that builds on this PR to enable the limited vector constant support.
